generatedSentence: In a distant future, the omnipresent networks began to surround every aspect of life, yet a complex patriarchy of elected officials maintained an eternal grip. A disaster was slowly brewing, an oxide vapor making the very air tepid and malleable, causing strange effects. One exception was the graphite-skinned protagonist, who felt a strange joy amidst the impending doom, often using darts to uncheck his frustrations. He wore a patched uniform, a stark contrast to the foreign machines that began to darken the horizon, sometimes even invoked by a rogue demon spirit. The threat felt like a jetliner crashing, causing everyone to become angry. The oppressive patriarchy's control became overtly ghastly, erecting gallows in the graveyard districts, pushing the populace into squalid conditions. The protagonist, a valiant guardian, often stood on a balcony, watching the children play a grim multiplayer game, their phantasy world a mirror to their reality. He possessed a charismatic presence, a lucid mind that questioned the universality of their suffering, launching an internal inquiry. He knew the regime was inbred, their majesty a façade, their policies hawkish. The leader's glassy eyes seemed to follow him even when he backpedaled, feeling momentarily ashamed of his inaction. He sought to orient himself within this galactic mess, knowing a frenzy was coming, like a combustion engine about to explode. Deciding to make his first move, he activated his hidden blaster, feeling the firm grip. A sudden rhapsody of alarms blared as he executed a swift backflip, using an old cushion as a springboard. He had to jostle past frantic maids and frightened goats kept as pets. His target: the central utility core. He had a packet of experimental alchemy materials, intending to cause a system knockout. His player skills from years of virtual tennis now translated into real-world combat, giving him an edge. He was poking at the system's weaknesses, a strategic upgrade to their defenses. He had to paint the new future, not just daydream about it. He felt like a rabbit caught in a trap, yet also a powerful iguana. The system began to urinate sparks, a nitrous gas filling the air as the old structure started to crumble. The snowman-like security bots were gone, their circuits rendered useless. He had taken down a ton of resistance, causing several strategic knockouts. He knew he would inherit a difficult new world, but he had pitched a future of unity. With a final kick and a victorious flirt with disaster, he saw the new lamps flicker on, illuminating the path to a better future. He grabbed his umbrella from the bowl of his ruined helmet, feeling the slight asperity of his victory. The jewels of freedom were within reach. He'd shown them how to knead out tyranny, a true hero to all. He never forgot the taste of haddock and pineapple in his moment of triumph.
panelSentences: In a distant future, the omnipresent networks began to surround every aspect of life, yet a complex patriarchy of elected officials maintained an eternal grip. A disaster was slowly brewing, an oxide vapor making the very air tepid and malleable, causing strange effects. One exception was the graphite-skinned protagonist, who felt a strange joy amidst the impending doom, often using darts to uncheck his frustrations. He wore a patched uniform, a stark contrast to the foreign machines that began to darken the horizon, sometimes even invoked by a rogue demon spirit. The threat felt like a jetliner crashing, causing everyone to become angry.

The oppressive patriarchy's control became overtly ghastly, erecting gallows in the graveyard districts, pushing the populace into squalid conditions. The protagonist, a valiant guardian, often stood on a balcony, watching the children play a grim multiplayer game, their phantasy world a mirror to their reality. He possessed a charismatic presence, a lucid mind that questioned the universality of their suffering, launching an internal inquiry. He knew the regime was inbred, their majesty a façade, their policies hawkish. The leader's glassy eyes seemed to follow him even when he backpedaled, feeling momentarily ashamed of his inaction. He sought to orient himself within this galactic mess, knowing a frenzy was coming, like a combustion engine about to explode.

Deciding to make his first move, he activated his hidden blaster, feeling the firm grip. A sudden rhapsody of alarms blared as he executed a swift backflip, using an old cushion as a springboard. He had to jostle past frantic maids and frightened goats kept as pets. His target: the central utility core. He had a packet of experimental alchemy materials, intending to cause a system knockout. His player skills from years of virtual tennis now translated into real-world combat, giving him an edge. He was poking at the system's weaknesses, a strategic upgrade to their defenses. He had to paint the new future, not just daydream about it. He felt like a rabbit caught in a trap, yet also a powerful iguana.

The system began to urinate sparks, a nitrous gas filling the air as the old structure started to crumble. The snowman-like security bots were gone, their circuits rendered useless. He had taken down a ton of resistance, causing several strategic knockouts. He knew he would inherit a difficult new world, but he had pitched a future of unity. With a final kick and a victorious flirt with disaster, he saw the new lamps flicker on, illuminating the path to a better future. He grabbed his umbrella from the bowl of his ruined helmet, feeling the slight asperity of his victory. The jewels of freedom were within reach. He'd shown them how to knead out tyranny, a true hero to all. He never forgot the taste of haddock and pineapple in his moment of triumph.
rawPanelSentences: In a distant future, the omnipresent **networks** began to **surround** every aspect of life, yet a **complex** **patriarchy** of **elected** officials maintained an **eternal** grip. A **disaster** was slowly brewing, an **oxide** **vapor** making the very air **tepid** and **malleable**, causing strange effects. One **exception** was the **graphite**-skinned protagonist, who felt a strange **joy** amidst the impending doom, often using **darts** to **uncheck** his frustrations. He wore a patched **uniform**, a stark contrast to the **foreign** **machines** that began to **darken** the horizon, sometimes even **invoked** by a rogue **demon** spirit. The threat felt like a **jetliner** crashing, causing everyone to become **angry**.

The oppressive **patriarchy**'s control became overtly **ghastly**, erecting **gallows** in the **graveyard** districts, pushing the populace into **squalid** conditions. The protagonist, a **valiant** **guardian**, often stood on a **balcony**, watching the **children** play a grim **multiplayer** game, their **phantasy** world a mirror to their reality. He possessed a **charismatic** presence, a **lucid** mind that questioned the **universality** of their suffering, launching an internal **inquiry**. He knew the regime was **inbred**, their **majesty** a façade, their policies **hawkish**. The leader's **glassy** eyes seemed to follow him even when he **backpedaled**, feeling momentarily **ashamed** of his inaction. He sought to **orient** himself within this **galactic** mess, knowing a **frenzy** was coming, like a **combustion** engine about to explode.

Deciding to make his **first** move, he activated his hidden **blaster**, feeling the **firm** grip. A sudden **rhapsody** of alarms blared as he executed a swift **backflip**, using an old **cushion** as a springboard. He had to **jostle** past frantic **maids** and frightened **goats** kept as pets. His target: the central **utility** core. He had a **packet** of experimental **alchemy** materials, intending to cause a system **knockout**. His **player** skills from years of virtual **tennis** now translated into real-world combat, giving him an **edge**. He was **poking** at the system's weaknesses, a strategic **upgrade** to their defenses. He had to **paint** the new future, not just **daydream** about it. He felt like a **rabbit** caught in a trap, yet also a powerful **iguana**.

The system began to **urinate** sparks, a **nitrous** gas filling the air as the old structure started to **crumble**. The **snowman**-like security bots were **gone**, their circuits rendered useless. He had taken down a **ton** of resistance, causing several strategic **knockouts**. He knew he would **inherit** a difficult new world, but he had **pitched** a future of **unity**. With a final **kick** and a victorious **flirt** with disaster, he saw the new **lamps** flicker on, illuminating the path to a better future. He grabbed his **umbrella** from the **bowl** of his ruined helmet, feeling the slight **asperity** of his victory. The **jewels** of freedom were within reach. He'd shown them how to **knead** out tyranny, a true **hero** to all. He never forgot the taste of **haddock** and **pineapple** in his moment of triumph.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a graphite-skinned individual, wearing a tattered uniform and a silver arm-blaster. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Graphite-skinned protagonist standing on a high platform overlooking a neon-lit futuristic city, a complex network of wires and dark vapor filling the sky, medium shot, eye-level. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist on a crumbling balcony, looking down at squalid conditions below, a ghastly gallows in the background, wide shot, high angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Graphite-skinned protagonist mid-backflip, firing a blaster, dodging robotic maids and goats in a chaotic utility core, dynamic close-up, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Graphite-skinned protagonist standing victorious amidst a sparking, crumbling server room, holding an umbrella, new lamps flickering on, medium shot, eye-level.

definitions.definition: Anything, such as a fence or border, that surrounds something.
To encircle something or simultaneously extend in all directions.
To enclose or confine something on all sides so as to prevent escape.
To pass around; to travel about; to circumnavigate.
definitions.example: to surround the world

definitions.definition: A humanoid figure made with large snowballs stacked on each other. Human traits like a face and arms may be fashioned with sticks (arms), a carrot (nose), and stones or coal (eyes, mouth).
A score of eight, especially within one inning (in baseball) or on one hole (in golf, where it is also known as dogballs).
A playing card with the rank of eight.
An attractive but heartless man (similar to ice queen).
Cloudy diffused matter such as mist, steam or fumes suspended in the air.
The gaseous state of a substance that is normally a solid or liquid.
Something insubstantial, fleeting, or transitory; unreal fancy; vain imagination; idle talk; boasting.
Any medicinal agent designed for administration in the form of inhaled vapour.
(in the plural) Hypochondria; melancholy; the blues; hysteria, or other nervous disorder.
Wind; flatulence.
To become vapor; to be emitted or circulated as vapor.
To turn into vapor.
definitions.example: to vapor away a heated fluid

definitions.definition: To emit vapor or fumes.
To use insubstantial language; to boast or bluster.
To give (someone) the vapors; to depress, to bore.
Able to be hammered into thin sheets; capable of being extended or shaped by beating with a hammer, or by the pressure of rollers.
Flexible, liable to change.
definitions.example: My opinion on the subject is malleable.

definitions.definition: (of an algorithm) in which an adversary can alter a ciphertext such that it decrypts to a related plaintext
An allotrope of carbon, consisting of planes of carbon atoms arranged in hexagonal arrays with the planes stacked loosely, that is used as a dry lubricant and in "lead" pencils.
Short for graphite-reinforced plastic, a composite plastic made with graphite fibers noted for light weight strength and stiffness.
definitions.example: Modern tennis racquets are made of graphite, fibreglass and other man-made materials.

definitions.definition: A grey colour.
To call upon (a person, a god) for help, assistance or guidance.
To solicit, petition for, appeal to a favorable attitude.
definitions.example: The envoy invoked the King of Kings's magnanimity to reduce his province's tribute after another drought.

definitions.definition: To call to mind (something) for some purpose.
To appeal for validation to a (notably cited) authority.
definitions.example: In certain Christian circles, invoking the Bible constitutes irrefutable proof.

definitions.definition: To conjure up with incantations.
definitions.example: This satanist ritual invokes Beelzebub.

definitions.definition: To bring about as an inevitable consequence.
definitions.example: Blasphemy is taboo as it may invoke divine wrath.

definitions.definition: To cause (a program or subroutine) to execute.
definitions.example: Interactive programs let the users enter choices and invoke the corresponding routines.

definitions.definition: A marine fish, Melanogrammus aeglefinus, of the North Atlantic, important as a food fish.
A distinctive outfit that serves to identify members of a group.
Phonetic equivalent for the letter U in the ICAO spelling alphabet, informally known as the NATO phonetic alphabet.
A uniformed police officer (as opposed to a detective).
To clothe in a uniform.
Unvarying; all the same.
Consistent; conforming to one standard.
With speed of convergence not depending on choice of function argument; as in uniform continuity, uniform convergence
(of a polymer) Composed of a single macromolecular species.
(of a polyhedron) That is isogonal and whose faces are regular polygons; (of an n-dimensional (n>3) polytope) that is isogonal and whose bounding (n-1)-dimensional facets are uniform polytopes.
To remove a checkmark.
definitions.example: Uncheck the “save my password” box.

definitions.definition: (travel) To remove a previously checked-in passenger from the check-in system, allowing changes to the ticket.
Nitrous oxide.
The system in some racing vehicles which pumps nitrous oxide into the engine to improve performance.
Of, relating to, or derived from nitrogen, especially in which the valence of the nitrogen is lower than that of a corresponding nitric species
Of, or relating to nitrous acid or its derivatives
Having a sharp odor like ammonia or nitric acid
A fabric or structure of fibrous elements attached to each other at regular intervals.
Any interconnected group or system
definitions.example: A network of roads crisscrossed the country.

definitions.definition: A directory of people maintained for their advancement
definitions.example: To get a job in today's economy, it is important to have a strong network.

definitions.definition: A group of affiliated television stations that broadcast common programs from a parent company.
Multiple computers and other devices connected together to share information
definitions.example: The copy machine is connected to the network so it can now serve as a printer.

definitions.definition: To interact socially for the purpose of getting connections or personal advancement.
definitions.example: Many people find it worthwhile to network for jobs and information.

definitions.definition: To connect two or more computers or other computerized devices.
definitions.example: If we network his machine to the server, he will be able to see all the files.

definitions.definition: To interconnect a group or system.
To broadcast across an entire network of stations and affiliates at the same time.
An unexpected natural or man-made catastrophe of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction, loss of life or sometimes permanent change to the natural environment.
definitions.example: People would suffer disasters when society's morality degenerates.

definitions.definition: An unforeseen event causing great loss, upset or unpleasantness of whatever kind.
A pointed missile weapon, intended to be thrown by the hand, for example a short lance or javelin
Any sharp-pointed missile weapon, such as an arrow.
(sometimes figurative) Anything resembling such a missile; something that pierces or wounds like such a weapon.
A small object with a pointed tip at one end and feathers at the other, which is thrown at a target in the game of darts.
A dart-shaped target towed behind an aircraft to train shooters.
A plan or scheme.
A sudden or fast movement.
A fold that is stitched on a garment.
A fish, the dace.
A cigarette.
To throw with a sudden effort or thrust; to hurl or launch.
To send forth suddenly or rapidly; to emit; to shoot
definitions.example: The sun darts forth his beams.

definitions.definition: To shoot with a dart, especially a tranquilizer dart
definitions.example: They had to dart the animal to get close enough to help

definitions.definition: To fly or pass swiftly, like a dart; to move rapidly in one direction; to shoot out quickly
definitions.example: The flying man darted eastward.

definitions.definition: To start and run with speed; to shoot rapidly along
definitions.example: The deer darted from the thicket.

definitions.definition: (games) A game or sport in which darts are thrown at a board, and points are scored depending on where the darts land
To make dark or darker by reducing light.
To become dark or darker (having less light).
To get dark (referring to the sky, either in the evening or as a result of cloud).
To make dark or darker in colour.
To become dark or darker in colour.
To render gloomy, darker in mood.
To become gloomy, darker in mood.
To blind, impair the eyesight.
To be blinded, lose one’s eyesight.
To cloud, obscure, or perplex; to render less clear or intelligible.
To make foul; to sully; to tarnish.
An evil supernatural spirit.
A neutral supernatural spirit.
Someone with great strength, passion or skill for a particular activity, pursuit etc.; an enthusiast.
definitions.example: He’s a demon at the card tables.

definitions.definition: A form of patience (known as Canfield in the US).
Any of various hesperiid butterflies of the genera Notocrypta and Udaspes.
A binary chemical compound of oxygen with another chemical element.
Lukewarm; neither warm nor cool.
definitions.example: I'm drinking a cup of tepid water.

definitions.definition: Uninterested; exhibiting little passion or eagerness.
definitions.example: He gave me a tepid response to the proposal.

definitions.definition: A playing card.
(in the plural) Any game using playing cards; a card game.
definitions.example: He played cards with his friends.

definitions.definition: A resource or an argument, used to achieve a purpose.
definitions.example: He accused them of playing the race card.

definitions.definition: Any flat, normally rectangular piece of stiff paper, plastic etc.
A map or chart.
An amusing or entertaining person, often slightly eccentrically so.
A list of scheduled events or of performers or contestants.
definitions.example: What’s on the card for tonight?

definitions.definition: A tabular presentation of the key statistics of an innings or match: batsmen’s scores and how they were dismissed, extras, total score and bowling figures.
A removable electronic device that may be inserted into a powered electronic device to provide additional capability.
definitions.example: He needed to replace the card his computer used to connect to the internet.

definitions.definition: A greeting card.
definitions.example: She gave her neighbors a card congratulating them on their new baby.

definitions.definition: A business card.
definitions.example: The realtor gave me her card so I could call if I had any questions about buying a house.

definitions.definition: Title card / Intertitle: A piece of filmed, printed text edited into the midst of the photographed action at various points, generally to convey character dialogue or descriptive narrative material related to the plot.
A test card.
A published note, containing a brief statement, explanation, request, expression of thanks, etc.
definitions.example: to put a card in the newspapers

definitions.definition: A printed programme.
(by extension) An attraction or inducement.
definitions.example: This will be a good card for the last day of the fair.

definitions.definition: A paper on which the points of the compass are marked; the dial or face of the mariner's compass.
A perforated pasteboard or sheet-metal plate for warp threads, making part of the Jacquard apparatus of a loom.
An indicator card.
To check IDs, especially against a minimum age requirement.
definitions.example: I heard you don't get carded at the other liquor store.

definitions.definition: To play cards.
To make (a stated score), as recorded on a scoring card.
definitions.example: McIlroy carded a stellar nine-under-par 61 in the final round.

definitions.definition: Material with embedded short wire bristles.
A comb- or brush-like device or tool to raise the nap on a fabric.

A hand-held tool formed similarly to a hairbrush but with bristles of wire or other rigid material. It is used principally with raw cotton, wool, hair, or other natural fibers to prepare these materials for spinning into yarn or thread on a spinning wheel, with a whorl or other hand-held spindle. The card serves to untangle, clean, remove debris from, and lay the fibers straight.
A machine for disentangling the fibres of wool prior to spinning.
A roll or sliver of fibre (as of wool) delivered from a carding machine.
To use a carding device to disentangle the fibres of wool prior to spinning.
To scrape or tear someone’s flesh using a metal comb, as a form of torture.
To comb with a card; to cleanse or disentangle by carding.
definitions.example: to card a horse

definitions.definition: To clean or clear, as if by using a card.
To mix or mingle, as with an inferior or weaker article.

One of the officials appointed by the pope in the Roman Catholic Church, ranking only below the pope and the patriarchs, constituting the special college which elects the pope. (See Wikipedia article on Catholic cardinals.)
Any of a genus of songbirds of the finch family, Cardinalis.
Any of various related passerine birds of the family Cardinalidae (See Wikipedia article on cardinals) and other similar birds that were once considered to be related.
(color) A deep red color, somewhat less vivid than scarlet, the traditional colour of a Catholic cardinal's cassock. (same as cardinal red)
Short for cardinal number, a number indicating quantity, or the size of a set (e.g., zero, one, two, three). (See Wikipedia article on Cardinal number.)
(grammar) Short for cardinal numeral, a word used to represent a cardinal number.
Short for cardinal flower (Lobelia cardinalis), a flowering plant.
Short for cardinal tetra (Paracheirodon axelrodi), a freshwater fish.
(bow) A woman's short cloak with a hood, originally made of scarlet cloth.
Mulled red wine.
Card games
definitions.example: He's a fan of cards.

definitions.definition: A network of interconnected systems.
A collection of buildings with a common purpose, such as a university or military base.
An assemblage of related things; a collection.
An abnormal mental condition caused by repressed emotions.
(by extension) A vehement, often excessive psychological dislike or fear of a particular thing.
definitions.example: Jim has a real complex about working for a woman boss.

definitions.definition: A structure consisting of a central atom or molecule weakly connected to surrounding atoms or molecules.
A complex number.
To form a complex with another substance
To complicate.
Made up of multiple parts; composite; not simple.
Not simple, easy, or straightforward; complicated.
(of a number) Having the form a + bi, where a and b are real numbers and i is (by definition) the imaginary square root of −1.
(of a function) Whose range is a subset of the complex numbers.
Whose coefficients are complex numbers; defined over the field of complex numbers.
A curve, polygon or other figure that crosses or intersects itself.
One who lives forever; an immortal.
Lasting forever; unending.
Existing outside time; as opposed to sempiternal, existing within time but everlastingly
Exceedingly great or bad; used as an intensifier.
definitions.example: some eternal villain

definitions.definition: The act of excepting or excluding; exclusion; restriction by taking out something which would otherwise be included, as in a class, statement, rule.
That which is excepted or taken out from others; a person, thing, or case, specified as distinct, or not included.
definitions.example: That rule is usually true, but there are a few exceptions.

definitions.definition: An objection, on legal grounds; also, as in conveyancing, a clause by which the grantor excepts or reserves something before the right is transferred.
An objection; cavil; dissent; disapprobation; offense; cause of offense; — usually followed by to or against.
An interruption in normal processing, typically caused by an error condition, that can be handled by another part of the program.
To choose or make a decision (to do something)
To choose (a candidate) in an election
One who is elected.
(history) A social system in which the father is head of the household, having authority over women and children, and in which lineage is traced through the male line.
A power structure in which men are dominant.
The office of a patriarch; a patriarchate.
Displaying or feeling anger.
definitions.example: An angry mob started looting the warehouse.

definitions.definition: (said about a wound or a rash) Inflamed and painful.
definitions.example: The broken glass left two angry cuts across my arm.

definitions.definition: (said about the elements, like the sky or the sea) Dark and stormy, menacing.
definitions.example: Angry clouds raced across the sky.

definitions.definition: A jet-propelled airliner.
A feeling of extreme happiness or cheerfulness, especially related to the acquisition or expectation of something good.
definitions.example: They will be a source of strength and joy in your life.

definitions.definition: Anything that causes such a feeling.
definitions.example: the joys and demands of parenthood

definitions.definition: Luck or success; a positive outcome.
The sign or exhibition of joy; gaiety; merriment; festivity.
To make greatly distressed and/or a discomposed appearance
To be placed or place someone under the influence of a psychedelic drug
To streak; to variegate
To experience reality withdrawal, or hallucinations (nightmarish), to behave irrational or unconventional due to drug use.
To react extremely or irrationally, usually under distress or discomposure
A streak or variegation in a pattern.
(minced oath) Fucking.
definitions.example: You're getting on my freaking nerves!

definitions.definition: Freakish.
Fucking.
definitions.example: He was so scared he freaking ran into a police station.

definitions.definition: A foreign person, particularly:
A foreign ship.
Clipping of chamber foreign: an outhouse.
A foreign area, particularly:
Short for various phrases, including foreign language, foreign parts, and foreign service.
Located outside a country or place, especially one's own.
definitions.example: He liked visiting foreign cities.

definitions.definition: Originating from, characteristic of, belonging to, or being a citizen of a country or place other than the one under discussion.
definitions.example: There are many more foreign students in Europe since the Erasmus scheme started.

definitions.definition: Relating to a different nation.
definitions.example: foreign policy; foreign navies

definitions.definition: Not characteristic of or naturally taken in by an organism or system.
definitions.example: foreign body; foreign substance; foreign gene; foreign species

definitions.definition: (with to, formerly with from) Alien; strange.
definitions.example: It was completely foreign to their way of thinking.

definitions.definition: Held at a distance; excluded; exiled.
From a different one of the states of the United States, as of a state of residence or incorporation.
Belonging to a different organization, company etc.
definitions.example: My bank charges me $2.50 every time I use a foreign ATM.

definitions.definition: Outside, outdoors, outdoor.
A device that directs and controls energy, often in the form of movement or electricity, to produce a certain effect.
A vehicle operated mechanically, such as an automobile or an airplane.
(abbreviation) An answering machine or, by extension, voice mail.
definitions.example: I called you earlier, but all I got was the machine.

definitions.definition: A computer.
definitions.example: Game developers assume they're pushing the limits of the machine.

definitions.definition: A person or organisation that seemingly acts like a machine, being particularly efficient, single-minded, or unemotional.
definitions.example: Bruce Campbell was a "demon-killing machine" because he made quick work of killing demons.

definitions.definition: Especially, the group that controls a political or similar organization; a combination of persons acting together for a common purpose, with the agencies which they use.
Supernatural agency in a poem, or a superhuman being introduced to perform some exploit.
The system of special interest groups that supports a political party, especially in urban areas.
Penis.
A contrivance in the Ancient Greek theatre for indicating a change of scene, by means of which a god might cross the stage or deliver a divine message; the deus ex machina.
A bathing machine.
To make by machinery.
To shape or finish by machinery.
Wooden framework on which persons are put to death by hanging.
A wretch who deserves to be hanged.
The rest for the tympan when raised.
Suspenders; braces.
Any contrivance with posts and crossbeam for suspending objects.
The main frame of a beam engine.
A tract of land in which the dead are buried.
(by extension) A final storage place for collections of things that are no longer useful or useable.
Of or pertaining to milk, or the secretion of milk.

definitions.definition: Relating to a galaxy.
Enormous (in size or impact).
Glass marble.
Of or like glass, especially in being smooth and somewhat reflective.
Including a lot of glass.
Dull; expressionless.
definitions.example: the glassy eyes of a person in a trance

definitions.definition: (of water) Lacking any chop; smooth and mostly flat.
An inbred individual.
definitions.example: Since you all marry your cousins I bet you're a bunch of inbreds.

definitions.definition: Bred within; innate.
Having an ancestry characterized by inbreeding.
Describing a strain produced through successive generations of inbreeding resulting in a population of genetically identical individuals which are homozygous at all genetic loci.
To breed or reproduce with those that are related.
To breed with those that share common traits or qualities.
To produce or generate within.
An ancient Greek epic poem (or part of one) suitable for uninterrupted recitation.
A random collection or medley; a miscellany or confused string of stories, words etc.
An exalted or exaggeratedly enthusiastic expression of feeling in speech or writing.
An instrumental composition of irregular form often incorporating improvisation.
A marker pen used to highlight text.
A cosmetic product that reflects light, used to emphasize the contours of the face etc.
That which highlights or emphasizes something.
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.
The property of being universal, common to all members of a class
The state or condition of being useful; usefulness.
Something that is useful.
The ability of a commodity to satisfy needs or wants; the satisfaction experienced by the consumer of that commodity.
Well-being, satisfaction, pleasure, or happiness.
A service provider, such as an electric company or water company; or, the securities of such a provider.
A software program designed to perform a single task or a small range of tasks, often to help manage and tune computer hardware, an operating system or application software.

The number 1 or any element of a set or field that behaves under a given operation as the number 1 behaves under multiplication.
The peculiar characteristics of an estate held by several in joint tenancy.

The form of consensus in a Quaker meeting for business which signals that a decision has been reached. In order to achieve unity, everyone who does not agree with the decision must explicitly stand aside, possibly being recorded in the minutes as doing so.
An upward grade or slope.
An improved component or replacement item, usually applied to technology
definitions.example: With my phone company, I get a free upgrade every twelve months if I keep topping up 10 pounds a month.

definitions.definition: An improvement
definitions.example: The remarried couples among my friends often joked that their divorce and remarriage amounted to an upgrade.

definitions.definition: To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Up a slope or grade.
A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.
A device containing oil, burnt through a wick for illumination; an oil lamp.
A piece of furniture holding one or more electric light sockets.
To hit, clout, belt, wallop.
To hunt at night using a lamp; see lamping.
To hang out or chill; to do nothing in particular.
To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
Subjected to kidnapping
To prod or jab with an object such as a finger or a stick.
To stir up a fire to remove ash or promote burning.
To rummage; to feel or grope around.
definitions.example: I poked about in the rubble, trying to find my lost keys.

definitions.definition: To modify the value stored in (a memory address).
To put a poke (device to prevent leaping or breaking fences) on (an animal).
definitions.example: to poke an ox

definitions.definition: To thrust at with the horns; to gore.
To notify (another user) of activity on social media or an instant messenger.
To thrust (something) in a particular direction such as the tongue.
To penetrate in sexual intercourse.
definitions.example: If she smokes, she pokes.

definitions.definition: The act by which something is poked.
Drudging; servile
To cover or smear with pitch.
To darken; to blacken; to obscure.
To throw.
definitions.example: He pitched the horseshoe.

definitions.definition: To throw (the ball) toward a batter at home plate.
To play baseball in the position of pitcher.
definitions.example: Bob pitches today.

definitions.definition: To throw away; discard.
definitions.example: He pitched the candy wrapper.

definitions.definition: To promote, advertise, or attempt to sell.
definitions.example: He pitched the idea for months with no takers.

definitions.definition: To deliver in a certain tone or style, or with a certain audience in mind.
definitions.example: At which level should I pitch my presentation?

definitions.definition: To assemble or erect (a tent).
definitions.example: Pitch the tent over there.

definitions.definition: To fix or place a tent or temporary habitation; to encamp.
To move so that the front of an aircraft or boat goes alternatively up and down.
To play a short, high, lofty shot that lands with backspin.
definitions.example: The only way to get on the green from here is to pitch the ball over the bunker.

definitions.definition: To bounce on the playing surface.
definitions.example: The ball pitched well short of the batsman.

definitions.definition: (of snow) To settle and build up, without melting.
To alight; to settle; to come to rest from flight.
(with on or upon) To fix one's choice.
To plunge or fall; especially, to fall forward; to decline or slope.
definitions.example: The field pitches toward the east.

definitions.definition: (of an embankment, roadway) To set, face, or pave with rubble or undressed stones.
(of a price, value) To set or fix.
(of a card) To discard for some gain.
To produce a note of a given pitch.
To fix or set the tone of.
Having a slope.
definitions.example: a pitched roof

definitions.definition: Having a specified tonal range.
definitions.example: a high-pitched scream.

definitions.definition: Fought from predetermined positions at a specified time and place.
definitions.example: a pitched battle

definitions.definition: Covered in pitch.
A substance that is applied as a liquid or paste, and dries into a solid coating that protects or adds color/colour to an object or surface to which it has been applied.
(in the plural) A set of containers or blocks of paint of different colors/colours, used for painting pictures.
The free-throw lane, construed with the.
definitions.example: The Nimrods are strong on the outside, but not very good in the paint.

definitions.definition: Paintballs.
definitions.example: I am running low on paint for my marker.

definitions.definition: A face card (king, queen, or jack).
Graphics drawn using an input device, not scanned or generated.
Makeup.
Any substance fixed with latex to harden it.
To apply paint to.
To apply in the manner that paint is applied.
To cover (something) with spots of colour, like paint.
To create (an image) with paints.
definitions.example: to paint a portrait or a landscape

definitions.definition: To practise the art of painting pictures.
definitions.example: I've been painting since I was a young child.

definitions.definition: To draw an element in a graphical user interface.
To depict or portray.
definitions.example: She sued the author of the biography, claiming it painted her as a duplicitous fraud.

definitions.definition: To color one's face by way of beautifying it.
To direct a radar beam toward.
definitions.example: "We'll paint the target for the flyboys," the JTAC said.
